pr.title: Match OSLink, __ppc_eabi_init, convert Gecko_setjmp to inline asm, and bring in CelestialAmber's ctype match
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Converted several small runtime/Dolphin/MSL assembly stubs into source or inline-asm-backed C: matched __OSModuleInit in OSLink.c, added __ppc_eabi_init.c/.h with __init_user and _ExitProcess plus an inline-asm __init_cpp fallback, replaced MSL ctype.s with C toupper/tolower using named LowercaseTable/UppercaseTable rodata symbols, and renamed Gecko_setjmp.s to Gecko_setjmp.c using Metrowerks inline asm. obj_files.mk was updated to build these source files instead of the old asm objects where applicable.
what_changed: Deleted the hand-written assembly implementation of MSL ctype.s and replaced it with src/MSL/ctype.c containing C implementations of toupper and tolower.
Renamed rodata symbols lbl_803B8D30 and lbl_803B8E30 to LowercaseTable and UppercaseTable, and updated include/variables.h comments accordingly.
Deleted asm/dolphin/os/OSLink.s and added src/dolphin/os/OSLink.c with __OSModuleInit implemented in C by zeroing __OSModuleInfoList.pLast, __OSModuleInfoList.pFirst, and __OSStringTable.
Introduced OSLink fixed-address declarations for __OSModuleInfoList and __OSStringTable using addresses derived from OS_BASE_CACHED, OS_MODULE_LIST_ADDR, and OS_STRING_TABLE_ADDR.
Moved Gecko_setjmp from asm/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.s to src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c while preserving the PowerPC instruction body as Metrowerks inline asm functions for __setjmp and longjmp.
Moved __init_user and _ExitProcess out of asm/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.s into C source; _ExitProcess calls PPCHalt and __init_user calls __init_cpp.
Kept __init_cpp as inline asm in the matching path, with a readable NON_MATCHING C loop over the constructor table lbl_803B7240.
Left the .ctors data section in asm/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.s while removing the matched text functions from that assembly file.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.o, src/MSL/ctype.o, src/dolphin/os/OSLink.o, and src/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.o.
smart_moves: Used simple semantic C for ctype once the table symbols were named, preserving the important MSL behavior that EOF/-1 returns -1 and other inputs are masked with c & 0xFF.
Renamed anonymous rodata labels to purpose-specific names before using them from C, making the decomp clearer and reducing dependence on raw lbl_ addresses.
Converted Gecko_setjmp to a C file without trying to force fragile compiler-generated output; inline asm preserved exact register save/restore behavior for low-level control-flow routines.
For __ppc_eabi_init, separated a readable NON_MATCHING constructor loop from the matching inline-asm implementation, documenting intended semantics while keeping the build matching.
Modeled OSLink's memory-mapped globals as fixed-address C declarations rather than leaving the tiny initialization routine in assembly.
Updated obj_files.mk alongside each source conversion so the build switched from asm objects to src objects in the same PR.
decomp_lessons: Tiny assembly stubs are often good candidates for C conversion when their behavior is simple and the object ordering/build list is updated carefully.
For runtime primitives such as setjmp/longjmp, exact ABI and register behavior can matter more than source purity; inline asm is an acceptable bridge when full C matching is impractical.
When matching constructor initialization code, a NON_MATCHING C version can capture the intent of iterating over .ctors even if the matching version still requires inline asm.
Renaming rodata tables to semantic names enables cleaner C replacements and improves future searchability.
Fixed-address Dolphin OS globals can be represented in source with address annotations, allowing initialization code to decompile cleanly.
Keep residual assembly data sections, such as .ctors, in asm files when only the text portion has been matched or moved to C.
naming_conventions: The OS module queue type was named struct OSModuleQueue.
Fixed-address OS symbols were named __OSModuleInfoList and __OSStringTable.
Address constants used uppercase macro-style names: OS_MODULE_LIST_ADDR, OS_STRING_TABLE_ADDR, and OS_BASE_CACHED.
The new init header used the path-local name src/dolphin/os/init/__ppc_eabi_init.h and declared __init_user and _ExitProcess.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used Metrowerks inline asm syntax with `asm int __setjmp(register *env)`, `asm void longjmp(register *env, register int val)`, and `nofralloc` to preserve exact assembly bodies.
Used `asm static void __init_cpp(void)` with `nofralloc` for the matching constructor-walk function.
Kept labels inside inline asm for __init_cpp, including lbl_8034CAFC, lbl_8034CB00, lbl_8034CB04, and lbl_8034CB10, matching the original branch structure.
Used an `#ifdef NON_MATCHING` block to provide an understandable C implementation of __init_cpp while keeping the default matching path in inline asm.
Represented toupper/tolower table lookup as `Table[c & 0xFF]`, matching the original clrlwi-to-byte-index behavior.
Preserved EOF handling in ctype by returning -1 immediately when c equals -1.
Moved matched assembly out of asm files but left unrelated or still-needed assembly sections in place, notably the .ctors section in __ppc_eabi_init.s.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace inline asm __init_cpp with fully matching C.
Find later PRs that rename lbl_803B7240 or formalize the .ctors constructor table symbol.
Find later PRs touching OSLink fixed-address globals or struct OSModuleQueue.
Find later PRs modifying Gecko_setjmp.c, __setjmp, or longjmp inline asm.
Find later PRs that add proper extern declarations for LowercaseTable and UppercaseTable outside variables.h comments.
